item.title: Slim README.md to storefront format — problem, solution, quickstart, links
item.intent: The README must sell the project in 60 seconds — everything else belongs in docs/. A 500-line README is a wall, not a welcome mat.
item.description: The current README.md is approximately 500 lines and contains content that belongs in 4 different docs/ files. After FEAT-014 through FEAT-020 create the destination files, the README must be cut to storefront format: badges, one-sentence pitch, one-paragraph description, The Problem (2-3 sentences from 'Why aishore?'), Quick Start (install + first sprint — 3 steps max, link to docs/QUICKSTART.md), How It Works (1 paragraph + pipeline diagram, link to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md), Status (honest alpha/beta/stable + what works/doesn't), Links section (all docs/ files with one-line descriptions), Author section (name, what you're building, open to roles). Remove entirely: How It Works detail, The Workflow, Autonomous Mode, Agent Roles table, Complete Flag Reference, Item Schema, Project Structure, Configuration section, Requirements, Keeping Updated. All of this content will already exist in docs/ files.
steps: Verify that docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/QUICKSTART.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/PROBLEMS.md, docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, and docs/CHANGELOG.md all exist (dependencies must be complete)
Keep in README.md: badges, opening tagline and paragraph, the flow diagram (You: define intent → aishore develops → You: review), 'Why aishore?' condensed to 2-3 sentences
Keep in README.md: Quick Start section but trim to just: install one-liner, init command, first sprint 3-step block, then 'See [full quickstart](docs/QUICKSTART.md)' link
Keep in README.md: How It Works but trim to 1 paragraph + the pipeline ASCII diagram + link to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
Add a Status section: current version, stability level, what works well, what's rough
Add a Links section listing every docs/ file with a one-line description
Add an Author section: Simon Plant, building AI infrastructure tools, open to roles, GitHub/Twitter links

Remove entirely: The Workflow (4 subsections), Autonomous Mode, Agent Roles table, Complete Flag Reference (all subcommand flag tables), Item Schema, Project Structure, Configuration (config.yaml + env var table), Requirements, Keeping Updated
Verify all removed content has a home in a docs/ file — nothing should be lost, only relocated
acceptanceCriteria: README.md contains only: badges, pitch, problem statement, quick start (abbreviated), how it works (abbreviated), status, links to docs/, author section
Every section removed from README.md has a corresponding docs/ file that contains the content
No content is lost — everything is either in README.md or linked from it via docs/
README.md links section includes: Architecture, Quickstart, Configuration, Problems, Roadmap, Contributing, Changelog
